name: IMA Brookwood Golf Club
about: IMA Brookwood Golf Club is a championship 18-hole course designed by Joe Szilagyi, established in 1938 and located in Burton, Michigan. The venue offers both outdoor play and indoor golf simulation services, featuring three indoor simulators equipped with FlightScope technology. It provides a picturesque and challenging experience with four sets of tee boxes, and is open to the public. The facility is designed for golfers of all skill levels, offering good speed of play and an enjoyable challenge alongside indoor simulator options for bad weather conditions.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to three premium FlightScope golf simulators for all-weather play
Four sets of tee boxes cater to all skill levels on the championship course
Active junior and First Tee programs for youth development
Established golf leagues and corporate outing options
Open-to-the-public policy with no membership requirements

food_and_drink.notes: No information on food service available
No information on alcohol service available

simulator_hardware.notes: Three indoor simulators equipped with FlightScope technology

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website doesn't specify whether club rentals are available for simulator use. It's recommended to call (810) 742-4930 to inquire about club rental options.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No information is provided on food or drink policies for the simulator facility. It's best to confirm with the venue directly.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes on a simulator?
faq.answer: The website doesn't specify typical duration for simulator rounds. With rates based on 1-4 hour blocks, a full 18-hole simulation would likely fall within the 1-2 hour range depending on group size.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is kid-friendly and offers a Junior Golf program. The First Tee program is also mentioned positively by visitors, indicating a commitment to youth golf development.
